q: In Power Automate, what is the main purpose of an expression?
o: To run functions that return/transform data beyond simple dynamic content
To assign security roles to a flow
To store a flow's run history
why: Expressions use functions to calculate, convert and compare values when dynamic content alone isn't enough.

q: Why do Power Automate expression docs often send you to Azure Logic Apps documentation?
o: Flows run on top of Logic Apps and share the same function reference
Power Automate has no functions of its own kind
why: Power Automate runs on Azure Logic Apps, so both use the same functions and the reference lives on the Logic Apps docs.

q: Decide whether each statement about writing expressions is true.

rows.s: Function names in expressions are NOT case-sensitive.
You can open the expression editor with the fx button or by typing '/' in an input.
A '?' after a parameter (e.g. format?) marks it as required.

why: Function names are case-insensitive; fx or '/' opens the editor; a trailing '?' marks an OPTIONAL parameter.

q: A simple setup to test expressions without other inputs typically uses which trigger/action pair?
o: A manual trigger with a Compose action
A Dataverse trigger with a Condition
why: 'Manually trigger a flow' + Compose is the recommended minimal harness for trying expressions.

q: formatNumber(12.5,'C') is used to display a value. What does the 'C' specify?
why: 'C' is the standard Currency format string, turning 12.5 into e.g. $12.50; an optional locale can change the symbol.

q: length('I love Power Automate.') returns 22. Why 22 and not fewer?
o: Spaces are counted as characters too
why: length counts every character in the string, including spaces and punctuation.

q: Evaluate if(greater(12,10),'Yes','No'). What is returned and why?
o: 'Yes', because greater(12,10) is true and if returns its first value
true, because greater returns a boolean
an error, because you can't nest functions
why: Read inside-out: greater(12,10) is true, so if returns the first value 'Yes'.

q: Besides an expression, what built-in flow action lets you write If logic without typing an expression?
why: The Condition action provides If/branching visually, an alternative to an if() expression.

q: When you drag dynamic content into a flow, which kind of function is Power Automate writing for you behind the scenes?
why: Dynamic content is backed by referencing functions like triggerOutputs()?['headers']?[...], written automatically.

q: coalesce(null, 'Power Automate', 'Power Apps') returns which value?
why: coalesce returns the first non-null value from the list.

q: Which function returns the ID of the current flow run, useful for logging?
why: The workflow() function exposes run metadata; workflow().run.id is the current run's ID.

q: Decide whether each statement about expression categories is true.

rows.s: A complex expression nests functions, using one function's output as another's input.
add(add(12,13),15) is invalid because functions cannot be nested.

why: URI parsing extracts URI parts; complex expressions nest functions (e.g. add(add(12,13),15) = 40).
